title: Mouthwatering Crunchy Fujian Pork Skewers
description: A winey, crunchy, crowd-pleaser Chinese dish from Fujian province, featuring kabse spice and paleo-friendly ingredients, cooked in a cazuela.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together kabse spice, coconut aminos, rice vinegar, sesame oil, garlic, and ginger.
Add the pork pieces to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the marinated pork pieces onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Place the skewers in a single layer in a cazuela.
Bake the skewers in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until cooked through.
While the skewers are baking, prepare the garnish by mixing together chopped scallions and a pinch of kabse spice.
Remove the skewers from the oven and garnish with the scallion mixture.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with additional scallions if desired.
